description: SQL injection in login — sequelize.query template literal with req.body.email/password directly interpolated
SQL injection in product search — sequelize.query template literal interpolating req.query.q (criteria)
Reflected XSS — search query echoed in response without sanitization (same sink as JS-002)
Path traversal — res.sendFile with user-controlled filename concatenated to ftp/ base path
Open redirect — unvalidated to URL parameter used in res.redirect
Weak cryptography — MD5 (crypto.createHash('md5')) used for password hashing instead of bcrypt/argon2
Hardcoded admin credentials / weak hashing seeds in insecurity module
IDOR — user can access other users' baskets by manipulating basket ID (no ownership check before findOne)
Server-Side Template Injection — _username_ replaced into template via regex without sanitization
Information disclosure — error stack traces returned in HTTP responses
Weak PRNG — Math.random() used to generate captcha values
XXE — XML parser processes external entities in B2B order upload
NoSQL injection in product review update — operator injection in MongoDB-like query
Unrestricted file upload — extension/MIME validation bypassable
ReDoS — vulnerable regex in utility function
